title: lepechinia leucophylloides
intro: Lepechinia leucophylloides, also known as 'Mountain Balm' or 'White-leafed Mountain Balm', is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its volatile oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Lepechinia leucophylloides include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function. Lepechinia leucophylloides can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized for therapeutic purposes.
